# Economic factor templates for alpha-discovery GA warm-start (W3).
# Format: `<id>: <dsl-expression>`. Lines beginning with '#' are comments.
# All expressions are valid atx DSL (parse + analyze clean against the
# discovery Library). Fields used: returns, close, high, low, sector.
# Ops used: ts_std, ts_mean, ts_max, signedpower, zscore, rank,
#            group_neutralize, vec_avg (all in registry.cpp).

# ---- Low-volatility anomaly --------------------------------------------------
# Low-vol: short high-volatility stocks (negative sign -> low-vol is long)
lv1: -1 * ts_std(returns, 20)

# Low-vol conditioned: sector-neutral + signed-power shaping
lv2: group_neutralize(signedpower(zscore(-1 * ts_std(returns, 20)), 2), sector)

# ---- 12-1 momentum (skip most-recent month) ----------------------------------
# 12-1 momentum: 252-day trailing mean minus most-recent 21-day mean
mom1: ts_mean(returns, 252) - ts_mean(returns, 21)

# 12-1 momentum, sector-neutral
mom2: group_neutralize(ts_mean(returns, 252) - ts_mean(returns, 21), sector)

# ---- 52-week-high proximity --------------------------------------------------
# 52-week-high: close relative to trailing 252-day max (George & Hwang 2004)
h521: close / ts_max(close, 252)

# 52-week-high, cross-sectionally z-scored
h522: zscore(close / ts_max(close, 252))

# ---- Long-term reversal ------------------------------------------------------
# Long-term reversal: contrarian bet on prior 3-year return
ltr1: -1 * ts_mean(returns, 756)

# Long-term reversal, rank-transformed
ltr2: rank(-1 * ts_mean(returns, 756))

# ---- BAB (Betting Against Beta) via vec_avg ----------------------------------
# BAB: long low-beta instruments (below cross-sectional avg vol), short high-beta
# vec_avg broadcasts the cross-sectional mean of ts_std over all instruments;
# subtracting it yields a zero-mean long-short signal (negative sign: long low-vol)
bab1: -1 * (ts_std(returns, 252) - vec_avg(ts_std(returns, 252)))

# BAB sector-neutral variant
bab2: group_neutralize(-1 * (ts_std(returns, 252) - vec_avg(ts_std(returns, 252))), sector)
